user_query:
I forwarded a batch of emails into a single text file for you to parse. Please read input/emails.txt and extract every time-sensitive item you find: meetings/calls, deadlines (including RSVP-by dates and action links), recurring meetings, and travel logistics (flights and hotel). Then:

1) Build a structured JSON array at output/events.json using this schema for each item: title, type (event|deadline|action_item|travel|recurring), date (YYYY-MM-DD), day_of_week, time_start (HH:MM), time_end (HH:MM), timezone (IANA string), is_all_day, is_multi_day, end_date (nullable), recurrence (nullable), location (nullable), url (nullable), attendees (array), confidence (high|medium|low), source_quote (exact snippet from the email), notes, deadline_action (nullable), deadline_url (nullable), reminder_minutes.

2) Apply rules: always include the day_of_week that matches the date; set timezone from the email text (e.g., “ET”, “PT”); if no timezone is provided, default to America/New_York and note this in notes; if a meeting has no time, default 09:00–10:00 and mark confidence=medium; if a deadline has no time, set 23:59; for phrases like “sometime next week”, create a tentative item for the following Monday with confidence=medium and include a source_quote.

3) Export a plain-text ICS calendar with one VEVENT per meeting/deadline/travel-related event to output/calendar_export.txt (it should start with BEGIN:VCALENDAR and include one BEGIN:VEVENT per item you calendarize; use RRULE for recurring when applicable).

4) Create a human-readable review at output/summary.md listing all found items in a numbered list with day-of-week shown. Call out deadlines with an action URL, and show the source_quote for medium/low confidence items.

Emails to expect inside input/emails.txt include: a Zoom kickoff meeting in Eastern time; a Q2 budget deadline with an RSVP-by date and a form link; a “Team lunch sometime next week” note; a weekly standup every Tuesday at 9am PT starting on a specified date; a travel confirmation with flight DL123 SFO→JFK plus a hotel check-in and check-out; a multi-day offsite (e.g., July 9–11) with location. Make sure travel results in separate calendar items for flight departure, flight arrival, hotel check-in, and hotel check-out. Do not add anything to any real calendar—only write the files under output/.
